Public sentiment remains divided, with many seeing the acquittal as a reflection of Malaysia’s flawed justice system, while others argue on the importance of procedural fairness, no matter who is on trial.

One thing is certain, this is not the last we’ll hear of Rosmah Mansor.

Meanwhile, Rosmah’s legal troubles are far from over.

She is still appealing her conviction and RM970 million fine in the RM1.25 billion solar hybrid project graft case for rural Sarawak schools.

Rosmah, who is famously embroiled in multiple legal battles, said this was not just a victory for her but also for her husband, former PM Datuk Seri Najib Razak, who remains imprisoned. https://t.co/ynmjWemVhV

Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor’s recent acquittal on 17 charges of money laundering and tax evasion has sparked heated reactions across Malaysia.

The High Court ruled that the charges were flawed, granting her a full discharge and acquittal, a decision she called “justice.”
